title: Suez Canal Blockage — Ever Given (2021)
description: Container ship Ever Given ran aground blocking the Suez Canal for 6 days, halting ~12% of global trade. Electronics, auto parts, and textile suppliers in South/Southeast Asia faced severe delays into Europe and North America.
supplier_impact: Suppliers relying on sea freight from India, Bangladesh, Vietnam to Europe delayed 10-21 days. Air freight costs spiked 3x.
risk_rationale: High severity due to single chokepoint failure affecting global trade volume. Long lead-time products most exposed. Suppliers with <14 days inventory buffer critically exposed.
decision_taken: Activated alternate Cape of Good Hope routing for 3 high-priority suppliers. Placed emergency reorders for products with <7 days stock. Flagged 2 suppliers on temporary watch.
outcome: Reorders arrived within 18 days via air freight. Cape routing added 12 days transit but avoided stockout. Total excess cost: ~INR 4.2L across affected product lines.

title: Chennai Floods — Supplier Warehouse Disruption (2015)
description: Extreme rainfall caused severe flooding across Chennai. Multiple supplier warehouses and manufacturing units in Ambattur and Sriperumbudur industrial zones shut down for 2-3 weeks.
supplier_impact: 3 tier-1 suppliers (electronics components, packaging) fully shut. 2 tier-2 suppliers partially operational. Port operations reduced by 60%.
risk_rationale: Regional concentration risk triggered. Multiple suppliers in same flood zone with no geographic diversification. Low inventory buffers (avg 8 days) amplified exposure.
decision_taken: Switched 2 product lines to Pune-based backup suppliers. Placed 30-day advance reorders from unaffected suppliers. Flagged geographic concentration as systemic risk.
outcome: Stockout avoided on all critical SKUs. Backup suppliers delivered within 5 days. 2 Chennai suppliers resumed partial operations after 18 days.

title: Mumbai Port Congestion — Pre-Diwali Season (2022)
description: Severe vessel congestion at JNPT ahead of Diwali festive season. Container dwell times rose from 3 days to 11 days. Import clearance delays affected seasonal merchandise and electronics.
supplier_impact: Seasonal goods suppliers delayed 8-12 days. Electronics importers faced customs backlogs. Festive inventory arrival missed optimal window for 4 SKUs.
risk_rationale: Moderate disruption — predictable seasonal pattern, not a shock event. Impact limited to import-dependent SKUs. Domestic suppliers unaffected.
decision_taken: Expedited air freight for 2 highest-margin SKUs. Accepted delay on lower-margin seasonal goods. Added port congestion monitoring to pre-season checklist.
outcome: Air-freighted SKUs arrived 6 days early. Delayed goods sold at 8% markdown post-peak. No stockout but margin impact on 4 SKUs.

title: Rajasthan Supplier — GST Compliance Suspension (2023)
description: A key textile supplier in Jodhpur had GST registration suspended due to compliance irregularities. Could not issue valid invoices for 19 days, halting all purchase orders.
affected_routes: Road freight Jodhpur to Delhi NCR
supplier_impact: Single supplier responsible for 35% of ethnic wear category supply. No valid invoices issuable. All POs frozen for 19 days.
risk_rationale: High category concentration in single supplier. Regulatory risk underweighted in supplier onboarding. 35% category exposure is above acceptable threshold.
decision_taken: Emergency sourcing from 2 Surat-based backup suppliers at 12% cost premium. Initiated supplier diversification review for ethnic wear category.
outcome: Backup suppliers covered 80% of volume gap. 20% gap led to partial stockout on 3 SKUs for 6 days. GST suspension lifted after 19 days; supplier reinstated.

title: Punjab Farmer Protests — NH44 Blockade (2020-21)
description: Prolonged farmer protests blocked key national highway entry points into Delhi for months. NH44 (Delhi-Chandigarh) and NH9 partially disrupted, affecting freight movement from Punjab, Haryana, and UP suppliers.
supplier_impact: Agricultural input suppliers from Punjab delayed 3-7 days per shipment. Some freight diverted via Rajasthan adding 400km. Cold chain produce most affected.
risk_rationale: Prolonged duration (months) distinguishes this from a short disruption. Recurring delay pattern rather than single event. Cold chain exposure severe due to time sensitivity.
decision_taken: Shifted cold chain procurement to closer UP/Uttarakhand suppliers. Increased safety stock on non-perishable Punjab-sourced goods by 30%. Established alternate Rajasthan routing SOP.
outcome: Cold chain stockouts avoided by supplier shift. Non-perishable buffer absorbed disruption. Alternate routing SOP used successfully in 3 subsequent disruptions.

title: Vizag Cyclone Nivar — East Coast Port Shutdown (2020)
description: Cyclone Nivar caused Visakhapatnam port to shut for 4 days. Wind damage to warehouse roofing at 2 supplier facilities in the Vizag Special Economic Zone.
supplier_impact: 2 SEZ-based suppliers with physical damage. Port shutdown delayed 6 export-bound consignments. Inbound raw material containers stranded at anchorage.
risk_rationale: Physical infrastructure damage adds recovery uncertainty beyond port reopening. Dual exposure: port shutdown + warehouse damage at suppliers.
decision_taken: Rerouted 3 consignments via Chennai port (480km). Triggered insurance claims for 2 physically damaged suppliers. Extended payment terms to affected suppliers by 30 days.
outcome: Chennai rerouting added 4-day transit delay. Insurance claims settled in 45 days. Suppliers resumed full operations in 12 days.

title: Semiconductor Shortage — Auto Component Supplier Cascade (2021-22)
description: Global semiconductor shortage caused by COVID-19 demand surge and fab capacity constraints. Auto component suppliers in Pune and Chennai unable to manufacture ECUs and sensors.
supplier_impact: 4 auto component suppliers reduced output by 40-70%. Lead times for ECU-dependent products stretched from 30 days to 90-120 days.
risk_rationale: Upstream commodity shortage cascading to multiple tier-1 suppliers simultaneously. Long resolution timeline (months, not days) with no quick alternate sourcing available.
decision_taken: Placed 6-month forward orders with 2 less-affected suppliers. Prioritized allocation to highest-margin product lines. Reduced SKU count in affected categories by 30% temporarily.
outcome: Forward orders secured supply for priority SKUs. SKU reduction avoided excess inventory risk. Full recovery took 14 months.

title: Rejected: Air Freight Escalation for Low-Margin SKUs
description: During a moderate port congestion event at JNPT, the system proposed air freight escalation for all delayed SKUs including low-margin commodity items.
supplier_impact: Delayed import clearance for 12 SKUs across 3 margin tiers.
risk_rationale: Low-to-moderate risk. Air freight cost for low-margin SKUs would exceed gross margin on those products.
decision_taken: PROPOSED: Air freight all 12 delayed SKUs.
outcome: REJECTED BY HUMAN. Reason: Air freight cost exceeds gross margin on 8 of 12 SKUs. Only 4 high-margin SKUs warranted escalation. Rule added: never propose air freight for SKUs where freight cost >15% of SKU gross margin.
rejection_reason: Air freight cost exceeds gross margin threshold for low-margin SKUs. Only escalate freight for SKUs where air freight cost is below 15% of gross margin.

title: Uttarakhand Flash Floods — Mountain Route Disruption (2023)
description: Flash floods and landslides in Uttarakhand blocked NH58 and NH74 connecting hill stations to plains. Suppliers of Himalayan products (herbs, woolens, handicrafts) cut off for 9 days.
supplier_impact: 3 specialty suppliers inaccessible. Perishable herbal products lost in transit (could not be evacuated before spoilage). Woolen goods delayed 12 days.
risk_rationale: Mountain supply routes are uniquely fragile — single road dependency, no sea/air alternate. Perishable product loss is unrecoverable (no delayed delivery possible).
decision_taken: Wrote off perishable stock in transit. Placed advance order for woolens from Ludhiana backup supplier. Flagged mountain-route suppliers for mandatory 30-day safety stock policy.
outcome: Perishable loss: INR 1.8L. Woolen gap covered by Ludhiana supplier within 7 days. Safety stock policy implemented for all mountain-route suppliers.

title: Bangladesh Garment Supplier — Labour Strike (2023)
description: Widespread garment worker strikes in Dhaka's export processing zones over wage disputes. 60% of factories in Ashulia and Gazipur zones shut for 11 days.
supplier_impact: 2 RMG suppliers with active POs affected. 14,000 garment units delayed. Chittagong port backlog developed as factories resumed but freight piled up.
risk_rationale: Labour disruptions in Bangladesh garment sector are historically recurring (annual wage cycle). Predictable risk that should have been buffered. Post-strike port backlog extends total delay beyond strike duration.
decision_taken: Accepted delay on non-seasonal garments. Expedited 2 time-sensitive seasonal POs via air from Tirupur backup supplier. Added Bangladesh wage cycle dates to seasonal risk calendar.
outcome: Seasonal POs arrived on time via Tirupur air freight. Non-seasonal delay of 18 days accepted without markdown impact. Wage cycle calendar implemented.

title: Rejected: Supplier On-Hold for Single Minor Delay
description: A supplier from Noida experienced a single 3-day delivery delay due to a local road closure for infrastructure work. System proposed flagging supplier as on-hold.
supplier_impact: Single PO delayed 3 days. No stockout. Safety stock buffer absorbed delay.
risk_rationale: Extremely low severity. One-time infrastructure delay with no supplier fault. Buffer absorbed impact with zero business effect.
decision_taken: PROPOSED: Flag supplier on-hold pending review.
outcome: REJECTED BY HUMAN. Reason: Disproportionate response to a one-time minor delay caused by external infrastructure work, not supplier failure. Rule added: on-hold flag requires either 3+ delays in 90 days OR a single delay >14 days OR confirmed supplier-side operational failure.
rejection_reason: On-hold flag is disproportionate for a single minor delay caused by external factors. Threshold for on-hold: 3+ delays in 90 days, or single delay >14 days, or confirmed supplier operational failure.

title: COVID-19 Lockdown — National Supply Chain Freeze (2020)
description: National lockdown announced with 4-hour notice. All non-essential manufacturing and logistics frozen. Interstate freight movement halted except for essentials. 90% of supplier base non-operational.
supplier_impact: Near-total supply chain freeze. Only essential goods suppliers (food, pharma) operational. 90% of POs cancelled or suspended. No inbound or outbound movement for 21 days.
risk_rationale: Maximum severity — systemic, simultaneous, nationwide disruption with regulatory enforcement. No alternate routing possible. Only mitigation is demand-side management and cash preservation.
decision_taken: Suspended all non-essential POs. Prioritized cash preservation. Negotiated extended credit terms with 8 key suppliers. Shifted focus to essential goods categories for duration.
outcome: Cash runway preserved for 90 days. 6 of 8 suppliers agreed to extended terms. Recovery to 70% operational capacity took 45 days post-lockdown. 2 small suppliers went out of business.

title: Ludhiana Industrial Fire — Hosiery Supplier Destroyed (2022)
description: Fire gutted a major hosiery supplier's manufacturing unit in Ludhiana's focal point industrial area. Complete loss of production capacity. Supplier unable to fulfil any POs.
affected_routes: Road freight Ludhiana to Delhi NCR
supplier_impact: Supplier responsible for 28% of winter hosiery category. Complete production loss. Active PO worth INR 12L unfulfillable. 4-6 months for facility reconstruction.
risk_rationale: Supplier-side catastrophic failure with no near-term recovery. High category concentration (28%) with no immediate backup at equivalent capacity and quality.
decision_taken: Distributed hosiery POs across 3 Tirupur and 2 Ludhiana alternate suppliers. Filed insurance claim. Accepted 15% cost premium for emergency sourcing. Initiated supplier diversification for all categories >20% single-supplier share.
outcome: Gap covered within 14 days at 15% cost premium. Insurance claim partially recovered INR 4.2L. Category diversification policy implemented — no single supplier >20% category share.

title: Iran-Israel Tensions — Red Sea Freight Rate Spike (2024)
description: Houthi attacks on commercial vessels in the Red Sea caused major shipping lines to reroute via Cape of Good Hope. Freight rates on Asia-Europe routes spiked 3-4x. Transit times increased by 10-14 days.
supplier_impact: Import-dependent suppliers (electronics, specialty chemicals) faced 10-14 day transit extensions and 3x freight cost increase. Suppliers passing cost through caused landed cost inflation.
risk_rationale: Ongoing geopolitical risk with no near-term resolution. Duration uncertainty distinguishes from one-off disruption. Cost inflation compounds with transit extension — dual impact.
decision_taken: Locked in forward freight contracts at pre-spike rates for 90 days where possible. Switched 3 product lines to domestic substitutes. Flagged cost inflation risk for margin review.
outcome: Forward contracts saved estimated INR 6.8L over 90 days. Domestic substitutes reduced import dependency by 18% in affected categories. Margin impact contained.

title: Delhi Pollution Emergency — Odd-Even Trucking Restrictions (2023)
description: GRAP Stage 4 pollution emergency declared in Delhi. Diesel truck entry into Delhi banned. Only CNG and electric freight vehicles permitted. Last-mile delivery severely impacted for 8 days.
supplier_impact: All diesel truck-dependent suppliers unable to deliver into Delhi. 3 warehouse hubs in Delhi affected. Last-mile delivery capacity reduced 65% for 8 days.
risk_rationale: Predictable seasonal risk (Delhi winters). Moderate impact — city-level, not national. CNG/EV freight alternate exists but limited capacity causes queuing delays.
decision_taken: Pre-positioned 12-day inventory buffer at Delhi warehouses ahead of forecast pollution season. Contracted CNG fleet for priority deliveries. Shifted non-urgent deliveries to NCR warehouses outside Delhi.
outcome: Pre-positioned inventory absorbed 8-day restriction with zero last-mile disruption. CNG fleet handled priority deliveries. Strategy added to annual pre-winter SOP.
